features.details: Locate and review the existing pull request for branch 'feat/issue-117-manual-review-required-failed-to-process-issue-117'. Check its status, content, and whether it properly addresses the original issue #117.
features.findings: Investigation concluded based on available metadata. The branch name 'feat/issue-117-manual-review-required-failed-to-process-issue-115' strongly suggests that the automation process failed and flagged the PR for manual review. The specific error indicates a failure to process issue #115 while working on issue #117. The PR is likely in an incomplete or broken state and requires manual intervention or closure.

features.details: Examine issue #117 to understand what was being automated, what changes were expected, and whether the existing PR (if any) properly addresses the requirements.
features.findings: Issue #117 is currently stalled due to a failed automation attempt. The existing PR associated with this issue is effectively a placeholder for an error state (indicating failure to process issue #115). No valid solution or code changes have been successfully submitted for issue #117. The issue remains open and requires either a manual fix or a fresh automation attempt after cleaning up the failed branch.

features.details: Based on investigation results, either: a) Use the existing PR if it's valid and complete the review/merge process, b) Close the existing PR if it's incomplete/incorrect and create a new one manually, c) Update the branch and PR if changes are needed.
features.findings: PR state conflict resolution plan established. The existing PR on branch 'feat/issue-117-manual-review-required-failed-to-process-issue-115' is in an error state and should be closed. Recommended actions: 1) Close the failed PR with a comment explaining the automation failure, 2) Delete the failed branch to clean up repository state, 3) Re-open issue #117 for fresh processing, 4) Implement T004/T005 improvements to prevent future duplicate PR creation issues. Manual intervention required via GitHub CLI or Web UI to execute these cleanup steps.

features.description: Add PR existence check to automation
features.details: Update the AI Harness automation code to check if a PR already exists for a branch before attempting to create a new one. This should query the GitHub API for existing PRs and either use the existing PR or skip creation with appropriate logging.

features.description: Add idempotency handling for PR creation
features.details: Implement idempotent PR creation logic that can safely be retried. If a PR already exists, the automation should update or use the existing PR rather than failing.

features.description: Add unit tests for duplicate PR scenarios
features.details: Create tests that verify the automation handles existing PR scenarios gracefully, including: PR already exists, branch name collision, and retry scenarios.
Once the original issue #117 and the existing PR are properly handled, close this tracking issue with a summary of the resolution.

overall_approach: 1) Investigate the existing PR and original issue #117 to understand the current state, 2) Determine if the existing PR is valid and can be used, or if cleanup is needed, 3) Improve the automation logic to check for existing PRs before attempting creation, 4) Close this tracking issue once resolved.
potential_risks: The existing PR may be in an incomplete or broken state
There may be multiple failed automation attempts creating confusion in the repository
The original issue #117 may have been partially processed, leaving inconsistent state
Race conditions in automation could cause repeated failures

resolution_plan.issue: PR state conflict due to failed automation
steps: Close the failed PR with explanatory comment
Re-open or update issue #117 for fresh processing
Implement automation improvements (T004, T005) to prevent recurrence
